stocks.impact_theory: NVIDIA announced ramp-up in Blackwell AI chip production on April 3, 2026, ahead of schedule due to surging demand from hyperscalers. Economically, this implies accelerated AI infrastructure buildout, potentially adding $500B to global capex in 2026. Market psychology: Reinforces NVDA's moat in AI, triggering FOMO buying amid broader tech rally. Historical parallel: Similar to 2022-2023 GPU shortage frenzy. Analogies: 1) Gold rush for AI picks-and-shovels; 2) Like Intel's dominance in PC era but supercharged by AI.

news_impact_analysis.reasoning: Web searches reveal headlines from CNBC, Bloomberg on production news; X sentiment (semantic search: 85% positive posts, high engagement on #NVDA #Blackwell) shows retail excitement. Analyst upgrades from Goldman, Piper Sandler target $180.

potential_profit_risks.long_position: Buy/long for 10-15% upside in 1-2 weeks on momentum; target $160.
potential_profit_risks.short_position: Short if breaks $140 support, potential 5-8% downside on profit-taking.
potential_profit_risks.key_risks: Chip supply chain disruptions (Taiwan tensions), competition from AMD/Intel, overvaluation bubble burst.

stocks.justification: Multiple confirming sources, high X volume, historical pattern consistency.

stocks.impact_theory: Tesla reported Q1 2026 deliveries of 520K vehicles, beating estimates by 10% despite robotaxi delay rumors quelled by Musk tweet. Economic: Signals EV demand recovery amid lower rates. Psychology: Restores faith in growth narrative. Parallels: 2020 delivery beats sparking 700% rally. Analogies: 1) Phoenix rising from production hell; 2) Like Apple's iPhone launch cycles.

news_impact_analysis.reasoning: Reuters, Yahoo Finance coverage positive; X keyword search shows #TSLA trending with 70% bullish sentiment, memes on deliveries.

potential_profit_risks.long_position: Long targeting $310, 12% gain on robotaxi hype renewal.
potential_profit_risks.short_position: Short on any Cybertruck recall news, -7% potential.
potential_profit_risks.key_risks: Regulatory scrutiny on FSD, China competition (BYD), margin compression.

stocks.justification: Strong data but offset by ongoing volatility from Musk tweets.

stocks.impact_theory: EU court upholds $2B fine but delays App Store changes, easing immediate antitrust fears. Economic: Preserves high-margin services revenue. Psychology: Relief rally as 'sell the news' avoided. Parallels: 2021 Epic ruling dip/recovery. Analogies: 1) Storm in a teacup for walled garden; 2) Like telecoms surviving net neutrality scares.

news_impact_analysis.reasoning: WSJ, FT reports on appeal success; X posts mixed but shifting positive post-delay news.

potential_profit_risks.long_position: Long for steady climb to $265, 8% gain.
potential_profit_risks.short_position: Avoid or short if US DOJ case escalates, -4%.
potential_profit_risks.key_risks: Broader antitrust wave, iPhone sales slowdown in China.

stocks.impact_theory: SMCI wins $10B AI server contract with MSFT, tying to NVDA ecosystem. Economic: Fuels data center expansion. Psychology: Validates AI infrastructure play. Parallels: 2023 AI server boom. Analogies: 1) Plumbing for AI skyscrapers; 2) Like Caterpillar in construction surges.

news_impact_analysis.reasoning: Seeking Alpha, Barron's scoops; X semantic high buzz around #SMCI contract.

potential_profit_risks.key_risks: Execution delays, accounting issues recurrence, dependency on NVDA.

stocks.impact_theory: AMD unveils MI400 AI chip rivaling NVDA Blackwell at lower cost, with hyperscaler trials. Economic: Increases competition, potentially commoditizing AI chips. Psychology: Underdog rally. Parallels: CPU wars with Intel. Analogies: 1) AMD's Ryzen comeback; 2) Like Pepsi challenging Coke in cola wars.

news_impact_analysis.reasoning: TechCrunch, Anandtech deep dives; X keyword shows #AMD vs #NVDA debates, 60% favorable.

potential_profit_risks.long_position: Long targeting $190, 12% gain on market share hopes.
potential_profit_risks.key_risks: NVDA dominance, delayed shipments, high valuation.

stocks.justification: Promising but unproven vs NVDA ecosystem lock-in.
